full_prompt:
Design a rectangular aluminum-honeycomb sandwich equipment panel for a European telecom spacecraft bus. The panel is a sandwich of two AA 2024-T3 face sheets bonded to an Al 5056 honeycomb core with a 3/16-inch cell and 2.3 lb/ft^3 density. The governing standard is ECSS-E-ST-32-10C Rev.2 (15 May 2019). For qualification metallic hardware at design loads, ECSS Table 4-3 prescribes a design yield factor KY = 1.25 and a design ultimate factor KU = 1.25 applied on limit loads, with an additional model factor KM = 1.2 if analysis correlation is not demonstrated; this brief assumes a correlated FEM so KM = 1.0.

Geometry. Panel planform 1200 mm x 800 mm. Total thickness 25.4 mm, face-sheet thickness 0.4 mm per face, core thickness 24.6 mm. The four long edges are supported by aluminum edge-close-out frames bolted to the spacecraft primary cylinder at 48 M5 titanium fasteners on a 100 mm pitch around the perimeter. Eight equipment-box hard points (potted inserts, each 20 mm diameter, 24.6 mm deep through the core) are distributed on a nominal 400 mm x 300 mm grid. Each hard point carries a lumped mass of 3 kg representing an avionics unit. Inserts are modeled per ECSS-E-HB-32-22A pull-out / shear allowables but that standard is out of scope for pass/fail here — only the face-sheet and core structural state is judged.

Materials. Face sheets: AA 2024-T3, E = 73.1 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2780 kg/m^3, Fty = 290 MPa, Ftu = 435 MPa. Core: Al 5056 honeycomb 3/16-5056-.0007 nominal, effective shear strength L-direction 1.93 MPa, W-direction 1.17 MPa, bare compressive strength 2.55 MPa, density 36.8 kg/m^3.

Load cases. LC1 (quasi-static launch): simultaneous 12.5 g axial + 8 g lateral applied at each of the eight 3 kg equipment masses (total lumped mass 24 kg) with the perimeter fasteners fully fixed. LC2 (random vibration Miles-equivalent): 20 g out-of-plane applied to each equipment mass, enveloping a 0.2 g^2/Hz random input at the panel first mode (~80 Hz assumed). LC3 (thermoelastic): a uniform DeltaT of +80 C and -100 C between face sheets (front-sun / back-shade orbit case); only bending-stress margin is judged. LC4 (buckling): compressive in-plane load of 4000 N/m applied along the long edges (worst-case launch panel compression from flexure of the adjacent primary cylinder).

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Face-sheet maximum von Mises stress at limit load times KY = 1.25 must not exceed Fty = 290 MPa, i.e. stress at limit must be <= 232 MPa. (R2) Face-sheet maximum von Mises stress at limit load times KU = 1.25 must not exceed Ftu = 435 MPa, i.e. stress at limit must be <= 348 MPa. (R3) Honeycomb core shear stress at limit load times KU = 1.25 must not exceed the L-direction allowable of 1.93 MPa, i.e. shear at limit must be <= 1.54 MPa. (R4) Global linear buckling eigenvalue under LC4 must be >= 1.25 (ECSS ultimate FoS for stability of a correlated metallic structure, Table 4-3). (R5) Panel fundamental frequency with equipment masses attached must be >= 70 Hz to decouple from launcher primary modes (typical project gate; not mandated by 32-10C itself). (R6) Panel areal density must not exceed 4.5 kg/m^2 including inserts and edge frame.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary analysis is a sandwich-shell linear static FEM (ESL laminate or solid-face-sheet + solid-core) for LC1, LC2, LC3, LC4. A linear buckling eigenvalue must be computed for LC4 and a constrained modal analysis for R5. Thermal analysis is limited to imposing the uniform DeltaT; no transient heat-transfer solution is required. Fluid and radiation analyses are excluded.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of panel + edge frame, areal density in kg/m^2, max face-sheet von Mises per case in MPa, max core shear per case in MPa, first buckling load factor for LC4, first five modes in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a rectangular aluminum-honeycomb sandwich equipment panel for a European telecom spacecraft bus. The panel is AA 2024-T3 face sheets bonded to an Al 5056 honeycomb core (3/16-inch cell, 2.3 lb/ft^3 density), carrying eight 3 kg equipment masses with perimeter fasteners fully fixed. Design to ECSS-E-ST-32-10C Rev.2 (15 May 2019), assuming a correlated FEM (KM = 1.0) with KY = 1.25 and KU = 1.25 on limit loads.

Envelope: planform 1200 mm x 800 mm, total thickness 25.4 mm, face-sheet 0.4 mm per face, core 24.6 mm. Four long edges supported by aluminum edge-close-out frames bolted to the spacecraft primary cylinder at 48 M5 titanium fasteners on a 100 mm pitch. Eight 20 mm diameter potted inserts on a nominal 400 x 300 mm grid.

Load cases.
- LC1 quasi-static launch: simultaneous 12.5 g axial + 8 g lateral at each 3 kg equipment mass.
- LC2 random vibration Miles-equivalent: 20 g out-of-plane at each mass, enveloping 0.2 g^2/Hz at the ~80 Hz first mode.
- LC3 thermoelastic: uniform DeltaT of +80 C / -100 C between face sheets.
- LC4 in-plane compression: 4000 N/m along the long edges.

Additional gates: panel fundamental frequency with equipment masses >= 70 Hz; areal density <= 4.5 kg/m^2 including inserts and edge frame.

prompt.title: Aluminum-honeycomb equipment panel under ECSS-E-ST-32-10C factors
prompt.brief: 1200x800x25.4 mm Al-sandwich panel carrying eight 3 kg avionics units; ECSS KY=1.25 and KU=1.25 applied to face-sheet and core allowables.

boundary_conditions.hard_points: lumped 3 kg each, rigid to face sheets

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Fty / KY = 290 / 1.25 (ECSS Table 4-3 metallic qualification yield)
Ftu / KU = 435 / 1.25 (ECSS Table 4-3 metallic qualification ultimate)

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: ESL composite shell or solid face + solid core

exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Ionizing-radiation degradation not judged; only structural state.
exclusions_explained.thermal_transient: Only uniform DeltaT imposed; transient orbital heat-transfer out of scope.

eval_coverage: multiple ECSS FoS applied at identical limit state
buckling + modal + static in one brief
